firma.heading: A Firm Built for
firma.historyBody1: FinancialQ Group was founded in 2000 on a simple but radical premise: sophisticated private investors deserve the same methodological rigor as large institutional funds — without the conflicts of interest that characterize traditional private banking.
firma.historyBody2: For 24 years we have built and overseen mandates for a deliberately small client base. We do not manage assets by scale. We manage commitments by depth.
firma.legalBody: We operate under the most demanding framework available to investment advisors in the United States.

items.title: Registered Investment Advisor
items.body: Regulated by the SEC under the Investment Advisers Act of 1940. Full fiduciary obligation to each client.

items.title: Fee-Only Model
items.body: No commissions, no product incentives. Only fees agreed directly with the client.

items.title: Independent Custody
items.body: Client assets are held at independent custodians completely separate from the firm's operations.

items.title: Professional Memberships
items.body: CFA Institute · CFP Board · NAPFA (National Association of Personal Financial Advisors)

equipo.heading: Institutional Depth.
equipo.intro: Our principals bring decades of institutional investment, tax and wealth planning experience to a deliberately small, focused and conflict-free firm.

members.name: Marcus Quinlan
members.bio: Former institutional portfolio manager with experience at a major university endowment fund. 24 years in highly complex wealth management. Specialist in regime-sensitive allocation and mandate architecture.

members.name: Elena Laurente
members.bio: Over 20 years advising UHNW families on cross-border planning, concentrated capital disposition and multigenerational governance frameworks. Fluent in Spanish, English and Portuguese.

members.name: Daniel Krebs
members.bio: Quantitative risk specialist with training in institutional factor modeling. Responsible for explicit risk budgeting and continuous monitoring of mandate compliance for each client.

members.name: Sophia Andros
members.bio: Fluent in four languages. Advises cross-border entrepreneurs and mobile families on legal structure, tax compliance and capital coordination across multiple jurisdictions.

mision.heading: The Purpose Guiding
mision.missionText: To preserve and grow our clients' capital through structured investment discipline, without compromise on the fiduciary standard.
mision.visionText: To be the reference standard for institutional wealth management applied to individual mandates of exceptional complexity.

values.title: Structural Integrity
values.body: No commercial pressure justifies a recommendation that does not serve the client's interest. Our business model eliminates conflict — it does not manage it.

values.title: Discipline Over Convenience
values.body: Easy decisions are rarely correct ones. We prefer the discomfort of discipline to the comfort of improvisation.

values.title: Permanence Over Quarterly Performance
values.body: The objective is not to maximize a period's return. It is to build wealth that endures for generations with structural integrity.

values.title: Selectivity as Responsibility
values.body: Accepting clients we cannot serve well is not a service — it is a disservice. Our commitments are selective out of respect and principle.

filosofia.heading: Five Fundamental
filosofia.intro: Our investment philosophy is not a marketing document. It is the operational foundation of every portfolio, recommendation and commitment.

convicciones.title: Process Over Prediction
convicciones.body: We do not forecast markets — we build processes. Superior long-term results come from consistently applied disciplined decision frameworks, not foresight about future market states.

convicciones.title: Risk is Multidimensional
convicciones.body: Beyond volatility: we manage sequence-of-returns risk, liquidity risk, behavioral risk, tax drag and objective deviation — each with an explicit budget within the mandate.

convicciones.title: Structure Precedes Strategy
convicciones.body: Account structure, entity selection and asset location are as determinative as allocation. We design the complete architecture before selecting the instruments.

convicciones.title: Alignment Through Fees
convicciones.body: We are fee-only. We do not accept commissions or product incentives. Our compensation is structurally aligned with client outcomes — not transaction volume.

convicciones.title: Permanence as a Design Criterion
convicciones.body: We build for generational relevance. Every decision is evaluated for its contribution to lasting, compounding wealth — not quarterly benchmarks or short-term performance attribution.

framework.heading: Capital Architecture
framework.body: A four-pillar methodology for building, governing and adapting portfolios through market regimes. Grounded in institutional principles — applied with precision to individual mandates.

pillars.title: Mandate Definition
pillars.body: Rigorous mandate specification: capital purpose, return objectives, constraint limits and time horizon with institutional precision before any allocation.

pillars.title: Explicit Risk Budget
pillars.body: Risk is the primary currency of allocation. We build portfolios by explicitly budgeting volatility, maximum drawdown and correlation — not as afterthoughts to returns.

pillars.title: Regime-Sensitive Allocation
pillars.body: Economic regimes govern asset behavior. Our framework is calibrated to macroeconomic cycles, incorporating regime identification as a permanent structural input.

pillars.title: Capitalization Architecture
pillars.body: Long-term outcomes are engineered through tax-efficient structuring, behavioral discipline and systematic rebalancing — allowing compounding to operate without interruption.

riesgo.heading: Risk is
riesgo.body1: Most advisors manage risk as if it exists in only one dimension: volatility. We identify and manage six dimensions simultaneously.
riesgo.body2: Each dimension receives an explicit budget within the client's mandate. There is no 'accidental' risk in a FinancialQ portfolio — every risk present was consciously accepted.

dimensions.title: Market Risk
dimensions.body: Portfolio volatility and drawdown. The most measured dimension — and the least sufficient on its own for complete management.

dimensions.title: Sequence Risk
dimensions.body: The timing of losses matters as much as their magnitude. Critically important in distribution and withdrawal phases.

dimensions.title: Liquidity Risk
dimensions.body: The ability to convert assets to cash at the needed time, at the right price and within the required horizon.

dimensions.title: Behavioral Risk
dimensions.body: Decisions made under emotional stress are the most frequent source of long-term value destruction in well-constructed portfolios.

dimensions.title: Tax Drag
dimensions.body: The cumulative tax cost of an inefficient strategy can outweigh the return differential between investment options over the long term.

dimensions.title: Objective Risk
dimensions.body: The probability of failing to achieve the financial objective defined in the mandate within the time horizon established by the client.

clientes.heading: Designed for Exceptional
clientes.body1: We work with a limited number of clients whose financial complexity demands institutional discipline — not retail solutions. Each relationship begins with a thorough mandate assessment.
clientes.body2: Our commitments are selective by design. We do not accept clients we cannot serve well — it is a responsibility, not a commercial restriction.

profiles.title: High-Income Professionals
profiles.body: Senior executives, partners and specialists with complex compensation demanding structured planning across multiple decades.
items: Equity compensation, RSUs and stock options
Coordination with corporate benefits and deferred compensation

profiles.title: Cross-Border Entrepreneurs
profiles.body: Business owners across multiple jurisdictions requiring integrated capital supervision and repatriation planning.

profiles.title: International Investors
profiles.body: Globally mobile families and principals with U.S. and international capital seeking disciplined allocation with fiduciary rigor.
items: Advisory available in ES, EN, FR, PT

profiles.title: Founders with Liquidity Event
profiles.body: Post-exit founders converting concentrated, illiquid wealth into durable, multigenerational capital with full clarity.

steps.title: Introductory Dialogue
steps.body: A confidential, no-obligation conversation to understand your circumstances and whether our approach is the right fit. No presentations, no sales pitch.

steps.title: Mandate Assessment
steps.body: Structured diagnosis of your capital position, objectives, risk parameters and structural considerations. Result: a clear articulation of whether we can serve your mandate.

steps.title: Architecture Proposal
steps.body: A bespoke proposal with capital architecture, fee structure and governance framework — before any decision is required from either party.

insights.title: Regime Identification as Portfolio Input: Beyond Rolling Correlation
insights.body: Macroeconomic regimes — not calendar periods — govern asset return distributions. We examine how identifying regime transitions improves portfolio resilience without sacrificing structural allocation discipline.

insights.title: Permanence as a Design Criterion: A Defense of the Generational Horizon
insights.body: Why optimizing for multigenerational wealth requires actively rejecting quarterly benchmarks and short-term incentives.

insights.title: The Implicit Mandate Problem: Why Ambiguity Destroys Value
insights.body: When the purpose of capital is not explicitly articulated, decisions are made against an imaginary mandate that no one agreed upon.

insights.title: The Arithmetic of Tax Drag on Long-Term Capitalization
insights.body: How pre-tax return assumptions systematically overestimate wealth outcomes — and a framework for incorporating post-tax compounding from mandate inception.

insights.title: Risk Budget Construction: Methodology and Limits
insights.body: A technical walkthrough of explicit risk budgeting: what it measures, what it does not, and how it integrates with mandate objectives.

insights.title: Stress Regime Correlations: Why Diversification Models Fail
insights.body: Cross-asset correlations tend to converge toward 1.0 precisely when diversification is most needed. Implications for portfolio construction.

insights.title: Concentrated Capital: Discipline, Diversification and the Cost of Inaction
insights.body: For founders with liquidity events, the greatest risk is rarely market risk — it is position risk disguised as conviction.

insights.title: Q3 2024: Regime in Transition and Portfolio Positioning
insights.body: Our perspective on the ongoing macroeconomic regime transition and its effect on the structural positioning of client mandates.

insights.title: Cross-Border Planning in 2024: Regulatory Changes That Matter
insights.body: A summary of the most relevant international tax regulatory changes for investors with active cross-border structures in the U.S.

newsletter.heading: Would you like to receive our
newsletter.note: Relevant perspectives only. No spam. Unsubscribe anytime.

governance.heading: The Fiduciary Standard.
governance.obligationBody: As a registered investment advisor, we are legally and ethically obligated to act in your interest — not our own. This is not a positioning statement. It is the structural foundation of every commitment we undertake.

obligations.title: Advice Free of Conflicts of Interest
obligations.body: We have no proprietary products, accept no third-party compensation, and maintain no referral incentives that could compromise our advice.

obligations.title: Transparent Fee Disclosure
obligations.body: All fees, methodologies and cost structures are fully disclosed before engagement. No surprises.

obligations.title: Continuous Mandate Review
obligations.body: Portfolios are reviewed against mandate parameters at defined intervals. Regime changes trigger structured reassessment — not reactive repositioning.

obligations.title: Regulatory Compliance Architecture
obligations.body: Fully compliant with SEC regulations. Independent custodians guarantee complete segregation of client assets from firm operations.

feeOnly.label: ✓ Fee-Only (FinancialQ Group)
items: Fees charged exclusively to the client

Recommendations based solely on the client's interest

feeBased.label: ⚠ Fee-Based (traditional industry)
items: Potential income from selling specific products
Structural and frequent conflicts of interest
Incentives that do not always align with the client

form.intro: Share a brief overview of your situation. We will review your inquiry and respond within 48 business hours to determine whether we can be of assistance.
form.placeholderSituation: Briefly describe what brings you here. No detail required — just enough context to understand whether we are the right fit.
form.privacy: Your information is completely confidential. We do not share data with third parties. We respond within 48 business hours.

footer.tagline: Fiduciary capital supervision platform for sophisticated investors. SEC-Registered. Fee-only. Conflict-free.
footer.disclosureShort: FinancialQ Group is a registered investment advisor. All investments involve risk.
footer.disclosureFull: FinancialQ Group is a registered investment advisor. Registration does not imply a certain level of skill or training. Past performance is not indicative of future results. This website is for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice. All investments involve risk, including possible loss of principal.
